member.office_address: 172 Cannon House Office Building Washington DC 20515-0506

form_analysis.form_header_text: FY 2027 Appropriations Request Form - Congressman Ami Bera
form_analysis.form_instructions_text: Every year, Congressman Bera is proud to advocate for the funding of federal programs that support California's 6th District through the annual appropriations process. If your organization participates in any federally funded programs, Rep. Bera can advocate to maintain or increase the total funding for these programs. Please fill out this form with your requests for programmatic funding, bill language, or report language. Please note that these appropriations requests cannot guarantee funding for any specific entity or project and requests must comply with all current House rules. Requests will only be accepted through this form. Please fill out one form for each request. A receipt of your request will be emailed to you. This form is NOT for FY2027 Community Project Funding requests. If you have any questions, please contact Asha Samuel (Asha.Samuel@mail.house.gov) or call our office at 202-225-5716.

fields.label: Will there be/is there an existing member support letter?
fields.rationale: The reference values indicate that an existing letter exists for the House, led by Reps. Don Bacon and Judy Chu.

fields.label: Which member is/will be leading the letter?
fields.draft_value: Reps. Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA)
fields.rationale: Referencing the current leads for the House NASA Science programmatic funding letter as identified in the reference context.

fields.label: Agency & Program Name
fields.draft_value:
* Agency: National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA)
* Program: Science Mission Directorate (SMD)
fields.rationale: This value correctly identifies the federal agency and specific account/directorate as requested in the reference context.

fields.label: Please share the e-Dear Colleague or Quill link. If not yet available, please edit or follow up once it is.
fields.rationale: The specific URL for the current session's Dear Colleague letter is not provided in the reference context; this requires submitter-specific knowledge.

fields.label: Appropriations Subcommittee
options: Agriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration, and Related Agencies
Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
Energy and Water Development, and Related Agencies
Labor, Health and Human Services, Education, and Related Agencies
Military Construction, Veterans Affairs, and Related Agencies
State, Foreign Operations, and Related Programs
Transportation, and Housing and Urban Development, and Related Agencies
fields.draft_value: Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
fields.rationale: NASA Science Mission Directorate funding is under the jurisdiction of the CJS subcommittee.

fields.label: Agency
fields.rationale: Standard agency for Science Mission Directorate requests.

fields.label: Account
fields.rationale: Standard appropriations account for the Science Mission Directorate.

fields.label: Priority of Request (If multiple submissions)
fields.rationale: Internal priority ranking is determined by the submitter's specific office and cannot be provided by the consultant.

fields.label: Type of Request
fields.rationale: This is a request for a specific funding level ($9 billion) for an existing program, which is a programmatic request.

fields.label: Description of Programmatic Request
fields.draft_value:
* This request provides $9,000,000,000 for NASA's Science Mission Directorate to support the agency's portfolio of robotic exploration and scientific discovery.
* Funding enables the execution of priorities identified in the National Academies' Decadal Surveys for Earth Science, Planetary Science, and Astrophysics.
* Sustains the specialized aerospace workforce and industrial base required to maintain American leadership in space.
fields.rationale: Utilizes the $9 billion funding ask and program descriptions provided in the advocacy and reference values.

fields.label: Total Amount of Programmatic Request - FY2027
fields.rationale: Using the specific funding ask amount for the Science Mission Directorate as provided in the asks_config.

fields.label: FY2026 Enacted Level
fields.help_text: Please provide the numeric dollar amount enacted for the current/previous fiscal year.
fields.rationale: Using the pre-filled FY2026 enacted level for the Science Mission Directorate from the reference values.

fields.label: Amount of Programmatic Request for FY2027
fields.rationale: The requested $9 billion for FY2027 represents a $1.75 billion (24%) increase over the FY2026 enacted level of $7.25 billion.

fields.label: Please explain how the request will benefit California's 6th Congressional District or the Sacramento region/California.
fields.draft_value:
* Strengthens California's aerospace leadership by supporting the broader statewide network of contractors and research institutions contributing to NASA missions.
* Promotes regional economic growth through NASA's documented 3-to-1 return on investment, which fuels innovation in high-tech sectors throughout the state.
* Inspires the future STEM workforce in the Sacramento region by funding high-profile missions that demonstrate American scientific achievement.
fields.rationale: As CA-6 has $0 in direct NASA Science spending in the provided context, the response focuses on the broader California aerospace ecosystem and regional workforce benefits as instructed.

fields.label: Justification for Request
fields.draft_value:
* Maintains U.S. preeminence in space science in the face of rapid expansion by international competitors, notably China's lunar and planetary programs.
* Provides necessary funding stability to prevent the further erosion of the specialized aerospace workforce following recent budgetary volatility.
* Ensures NASA can continue to address the most urgent scientific questions about our planet and solar system as defined by peer-reviewed Decadal Surveys.
fields.rationale: Draws on strategic advocacy talking points regarding international competition, workforce stability, and the value of scientific roadmaps.

fields.label: Upload any additional documents that support your request
fields.rationale: File must be attached manually.

fields.label: Language Request
fields.rationale: The request context indicates that no specific bill or report language is requested (N/A) as this is a programmatic funding level request.

fields.label: Language being requested
fields.rationale: This is a programmatic funding request, and the context indicates no specific bill or report language is being requested.

fields.label: Please explain how the request will benefit California's 6th Congressional District or the Sacramento region/California.
fields.rationale: This field is part of a language-specific request section which is not applicable for this programmatic funding request.

fields.label: Justification for Request
fields.rationale: This field is part of a language-specific request section which is not applicable for this programmatic funding request.

fields.label: Has this language been included in previous Fiscal Years?  If so, please provide reference information below. Please include the fiscal year and bill number where the language was included.
fields.rationale: Information regarding prior year submission history for this specific constituent is not available in the provided context.

fields.rationale: File must be attached manually.

form_analysis.deadline_text: March 6, 2026 (Ag-FDA, CJS, FSGG, LegBranch, MilCon-VA, NatSec); March 13, 2026 (Defense, Energy & Water, Homeland, Interior, Labor-HHS, T-HUD)
